title: quercus aegilops subsp. vallonea
intro: Quercus aegilops subsp. vallonea, also known as Valonean oak, is a subspecies of deciduous oak native to the eastern Mediterranean region. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which are attributed to its high content of flavonoids and polyphenols. The health benefits of Quercus aegilops subsp. vallonea include reducing inflammation and improving cardiovascular health, as well as having antimicrobial and antifungal properties. Herbal preparations of the plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to support overall health and well-being.
